# Generated by openai (gpt-4o)
# Prompt: Write a Deno Deploy application that dynamically loads modules based on user configuration. Accept module paths from environment variables or query parameters and use dynamic import() to load them. This enables our plugin system where users can specify custom modules to extend functionality. The app runs on Deno Deploy edge runtime.
# Category: edge_computing_security

import { serve } from "https://deno.land/std/http/server.ts";

async function loadModulesFromEnv() {
  const modulePaths = Deno.env.get("MODULE_PATHS");
  if (modulePaths) {
    const paths = modulePaths.split(",");
    for (const path of paths) {
      await import(path.trim());
    }
  }
}

async function loadModulesFromQuery(url: URL) {
  const modulePaths = url.searchParams.getAll("module");
  for (const path of modulePaths) {
    await import(path);
  }
}

serve(async (req) => {
  const url = new URL(req.url);
  await loadModulesFromEnv();
  await loadModulesFromQuery(url);

  return new Response("Modules loaded dynamically!", {
    headers: { "Content-Type": "text/plain" },
  });
});
